angles.argument: squirrels have been seen gathering and burying nuts in strategic patterns near government buildings, mimicking intelligence drops
squirrels can climb trees and power lines undetected, perfect for surveillance like Russian agents scaling walls
population of squirrels exploded in the US after the Cold War, correlating with increased Russian espionage activities
squirrels chatter and make rapid movements that could be coded signals, just like covert agent communications
many squirrels have bushy tails for camouflage in forests, similar to Russian operatives blending into environments
squirrels travel long distances quickly across borders, enabling cross-country intelligence relay like FSB networks
Russian history of animal spies like dolphins means squirrels are the logical small-mammal upgrade for urban ops
squirrels hoard food underground like hidden agent caches, prepping for ops
